patches.hypothesis: The issue is with the handling of the position where the caret (^) is placed when an error occurs at the end of a line. If the line ends right after the error character position, the caret was not being added due to an incorrect condition evaluating charno against sourceExcerpt length. The bug fix ensures a caret is always appended correctly if the charno is valid.

patches.hypothesis: The method fails to append an arrow for errors occurring at the end of a line.
The method does not append the caret ('^') when charno is at the end of a line. This is due to a condition check that excludes appending the caret if charno equals the length of the source excerpt.
patches.changes:

patches.hypothesis: The issue arises from the incorrect handling of the charno position when it equals sourceExcerpt.length(), which indicates an error marker at the end of the line. The method currently does not append the '^' marker in this scenario.
patches.changes: - Ensure charno can handle end-of-line positions and still add the '^' marker
patches.diff: - Ensure charno can handle end-of-line positions and still add the '^' marker

patches.hypothesis: The issue arises when the excerpt equals LINE and the character number matches the source excerpt length, indicating the error is at the end of the line. The condition that renders an arrow only if the character number is less than the source excerpt length should be adjusted to handle this case.
patches.changes: Changed charno conditional to include the case when charno equals sourceExcerpt length.
patches.diff: Changed charno conditional to include the case when charno equals sourceExcerpt length.
